Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[Category:dd]][[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[Category:dd]] [[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:dardar]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

revision.comment: some pages here lack the tag but to my knowledge everything here eitherr is an original page or at least worth preserving while I potentially trirm the wikipedia derived articles
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

revision.comment: fixing error I did on all these pages
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

[[Category:bump]]
[[Category:ファイル]] [[Category:dardar]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages with redlinks to fill]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:ファイル]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}

[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}

At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted link categories]] to [[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:qq]] [[ja:大宮売]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Ōmiyahime-no-Mikoto]] to [[Ōmiyanome no Ōkami]]
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}
