[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōmiya-no-Me]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E8%B3%A3%E7%A5%9E]] [[ja:大宮売]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmiya-no-Me]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmiya-no-Me]][[Category:qq]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

[[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Ōmiya-no-Me]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:q--1]] [[Category:SuQqqqqq]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E8%B3%A3%E7%A5%9E]] [[ja:大宮売]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmiya-no-Me]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Ōmiya-no-Me]][[Category:qq]]

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}

revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōmiya-no-Me]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Heavenly deities]] [[Category:Female deities]] [[Category:Gods of prosperity]] [[Category:Gods of the arts]] [[Category:11-01-18]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya-no-Me]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E8%B3%A3%E7%A5%9E]] [[ja:大宮売]] [[en:Ōmiya-no-Me]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya-no-Me}}

revision.comment: Bot: Replaced ill templates with appended |12=draft and created corresponding redirect pages.
revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿|12=draft}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=draft}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=draft}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=draft}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王|12=draft}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=draft}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女|12=draft}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=draft}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学|12=draft}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=draft}}

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿|12=draft}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=draft}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王|12=draft}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=draft}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>

{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Ōmiya-no-Me]] [[Category:Shinto deities]] [[Category:Heavenly deities]] [[Category:Female deities]] [[Category:Gods of prosperity]] [[Category:Gods of the arts]] [[Category:11-01-18]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:to be resorted]] [[Category:return the see also]] }} [[sn:Ōmiya-no-Me]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E8%B3%A3%E7%A5%9E]] [[ja:大宮売]] [[en:Ōmiya-no-Me]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Omiyanome}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōmiya-no-Me}}

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿|12=draft}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=draft}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=draft}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=draft}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王|12=draft}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=draft}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女|12=draft}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=draft}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学|12=draft}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}}, she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of {{ill|palaces|ja|宮殿|12=draft}}.<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|12=draft}} or the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀|12=draft}}, but she appears in the {{ill|Kogo Shūi|ja|古語拾遺|12=draft}} as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=draft}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} when the latter moved from the {{ill|Ama-no-Iwato|ja|天岩戸|1=Amano-Iwato|lt=Heavenly Rock Cave|12=draft}} to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=draft}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=draft}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王|12=draft}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=draft}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女|12=draft}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=draft}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学|12=draft}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=draft}}

'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[deity]]. In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=draft}} found in the {{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式|12=draft}}, she is described as a deity who resides in the [[Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|ja|親王|12=draft}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} for the protection of the [[Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=draft}} enshrined at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}}, she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}}, the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified {{ill|miko|ja|巫女|12=draft}} who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in {{ill|Heian-kyō|ja|平安京|12=draft}}, eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period {{ill|kokugaku|ja|国学|12=draft}} scholar {{ill|Hirata Atsutane|ja|平田篤胤|12=draft}} argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=draft}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}}. At shrines like {{ill|Yūtoku Inari Shrine|ja|祐徳稲荷神社|12=draft}} and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=draft}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ({{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}})]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the {{ill|Jingikan|ja|神祇官|1=Department of Divinities|blank=|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft}}, and {{ill|Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kyōtango|ja|京丹後市|12=draft}}, the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} and other {{ill|Inari Shrines|ja|稲荷神社|12=draft}} across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher={{ill|Kōgakkan University|ja|皇學館大学|12=draft}}出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ame-no-Uzume|ja|アメノウズメ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Fushimi Inari-taisha|ja|伏見稲荷大社|12=draft}} * {{ill|Inari deity|ja|稲荷神|12=draft}} * {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|ウカノミタマ|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=draft}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Add wikidata]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

revision.comment: 50 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:オオミヤノメ up to rev 101833015
revision.text:
'''オオミヤノメ'''は、[[神道]]の[[神 (神道)|神]]。『[[延喜式神名帳]]』と『[[古語拾遺]]』では'''大宮売神'''（おおみやのめのかみ）、[[伏見稲荷大社]]では'''大宮能売大神'''（おおみやのめのおおかみ）<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref>と表記する。[[宮殿]]の平安を守る[[女神]]である<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009年。</ref>。
== 神話などでの記述 == 『[[古事記]]』や『[[日本書紀]]』には記されないが、『[[古語拾遺]]』に[[フトダマ|太玉命]]の子として登場する。[[天岩戸]]から新殿に移った[[天照大神]]に[[侍女]]として仕えたとされ、[[内侍]]（女官）が善言美詞（麗しい言葉）を駆使して君臣の間を和らげるような働きをしたと記されている。
『[[延喜式]]』の大殿祭（おおとのほがい）の[[祝詞]]においては、[[皇居]]に鎮座して[[親王]]や諸臣たちが過ちを犯すことなく心安らかに仕えられるよう見守る神であるとされている。
== 解説 == [[神祇官]]で祀られた[[天皇]]守護の[[八神殿|八神]]のうちの一柱として、[[朝廷 (日本)|朝廷]]で重視された女神である<ref name=inariookami/>。宮殿の人格化とも[[女官]]の神格化ともいわれ、上述したように君臣の間を取り持ち調和を図る神とされる{{Sfn|御巫祭神八座（式内社）|pp=15-16}}{{Sfn|神社の古代史|2011年|pp=186-187}}。こうした性格から、[[旅館]]や[[百貨店]]など接客業の守護神として信仰されるほか、家内安全・家族和合の神ともされている<ref name=tobe>戸部民夫『「日本の神様」がよくわかる本』、PHP研究所、2004年、pp.271-272。</ref>。
[[伏見稲荷大社]]で祀られる[[稲荷神|稲荷]]三神（上社・中社・下社の神の総称）の一柱でもあり、主祭神の[[ウカノミタマ]]の[[配神]]として上社に祀られている<ref name=inariookami/>。穀物神であるウカノミタマに仕える[[巫女]]を神格化したものともされる<ref name=tobe/>。こうした属性から[[平安京]]の官営市場の守護神として祀られた結果、商売繁盛の神としても信仰されるようになった<ref name=inariookami/>。
また、[[江戸時代]]の[[国学]]者・[[平田篤胤]]は『宮比神御伝記』で、[[伊勢神宮]][[皇大神宮|内宮]]に祀られる[[興玉神#宮比神|宮比神（みやびのかみ）]]は、オオミヤノメまたは[[アメノウズメ]]（芸能の女神）の別名であると説いている。[[祐徳稲荷神社]]や[[志和稲荷神社]]でもオオミヤノメをアメノウズメに当てており、技芸上達の神としている<ref>祐徳稲荷神社HP [https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/ お祀りする神様]、2016年4月15日閲覧。</ref><ref>志和稲荷神社HP [http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/ 志和稲荷神社について]、2016年4月16日閲覧。</ref>。
== オオミヤノメを祀る神社 == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|大宮姫稲荷神社（[[京都府]][[京都市]]）]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[大宮売神社]]（京都府[[京丹後市]]）]] 神祇官の故地に近い大宮姫稲荷神社（[[京都府]][[京都市]][[上京区]]主税町）{{Sfn|御巫祭神八座（式内社）|pp=21-23}}や、「大宮売」を称する唯一の[[式内社]]である[[大宮売神社]]（京都府[[京丹後市]]）{{Sfn|神社の古代史|2011年|pp=186-187}}で、主祭神として祀られている。そのほか、[[伏見稲荷大社]]（京都市[[伏見区]]）をはじめとする日本各地の[[稲荷神社]]で、ウカノミタマなどの配神として祀られる。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} <references />
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会編|author=|chapter=|year=1979|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[皇學館大学]]出版部|page=|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Wikicite|reference=「序説」|ref={{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}}}、{{Wikicite|reference=「御巫祭神八座」|ref={{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}}}。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=岡田精司|year=2011|chapter=神祇官の祭り -西院の神々と御巫の奉仕-|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=[[学生社]]|isbn=978-4311203022|ref={{Harvid|神社の古代史|2011年}}}} * 中村陽・監修『稲荷大神』、[[戎光祥出版]]、2009年。 ISBN 978-4-86403-003-8。 * [[戸部民夫]]『「日本の神様」がよくわかる本』、[[PHP研究所]]、2004年。ISBN 978-4569661155。 * 『お伊勢さん125社めぐり』別冊『伊勢人』、[[伊勢文化舎]]、2008年。 ISBN 978-4-900759-37-4。
== 関連項目 == * [[八神殿]] * [[アメノウズメ]] * [[伏見稲荷大社]] * [[稲荷神]] * [[ウカノミタマ]] * [[サタヒコ]]
{{神道 横}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:おおみやのめ}} [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:天津神]] [[Category:女神]] [[Category:豊穣神]] [[Category:商業神]] [[Category:芸術神]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. == References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}
