revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

== Mythological Descriptions ==
Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

== Explanation ==
Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>

As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome ==
[[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]]
[[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]]
Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== Further Reading ==
* {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}}
''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}.
* {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}}
* {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}}
* [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]
* [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]]
* [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]]
* [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]]
* {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

[[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]]
[[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

== Mythological Descriptions ==
Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

== Explanation ==
Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>

As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome ==
[[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]]
[[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]]
Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]]
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

== Mythological Descriptions ==
Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

== Explanation ==
Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>

As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okita-no-Kami|lt=Okita-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome ==
[[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]]
[[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]]
Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Amatsukami]] }}
[[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E8%B3%A3%E7%A5%9E]]

Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. [[zh:大宮賣神]] [[ja:オオミヤノメ]] {{translated page|ja|オオミヤノメ|version=101833015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q24865597}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. [[zh:大宮賣神]] [[ja:オオミヤノメ]] {{translated page|ja|オオミヤノメ|version=101833015|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{wikidata link|Q24865597}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}}
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>

== Mythological Descriptions ==
Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

== Explanation ==
Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>

As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome ==
[[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]]
[[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]]
Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:オオミヤノメ]][[zh:大宮賣神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:34, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:38, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Ōmiyanome''' (大宮売) is a [[Shinto]] [[Kami|Deity]]. In the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} and [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]], she is written as '''Ōmiyanome no Kami''' (大宮売神), while at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is referred to as '''Ōmiyanome no Ōkami''' (大宮能売大神).<ref name=gosaijin>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://inari.jp/about/saijin/|author=伏見稲荷大社 |title=伏見稲荷大社とは -ご祭神|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref> She is a goddess who protects the peace of [[:en:Palace|palaces]].<ref name=inariookami>稲田智宏「稲荷大神五柱とは何か」『稲荷大神』、戎光祥出版、2009th year 。</ref>
== Mythological Descriptions == Ōmiyanome is not mentioned in either the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] or the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]], but she appears in the [[:en:Kogo Shūi|Kogo Shūi]] as a child of {{ill|Futodama|ja|太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to フトダマ}}. She is described as serving as an attendant to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] when the latter moved from the [[:en:Amano-Iwato|Heavenly Rock Cave]] to a new palace. According to the text, she acted as a {{ill|court lady|ja|内侍|1=Naishi (court lady)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/court lady|13=User:Immanuelle/Naishi (court lady)|qq=}} who facilitated harmony between the emperor and his subjects through beautiful and wise words.
In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.
== Explanation == Ōmiyanome is one of the eight deities venerated by the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] for the protection of the [[:en:Emperor of Japan|emperor]].<ref name=inariookami/> She has been described as a personification of the palace or the deification of court ladies. Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>
Additionally, the Edo-period [[:en:Kokugaku|kokugaku]] scholar [[:en:Hirata Atsutane|Hirata Atsutane]] argued in his text "Miyabinokami no Dengi" that the deity {{ill|Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Okitama-no-Kami|lt=Miyabinokami|ja|宮比神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyabinokami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 興玉神}} of the {{ill|Inner Shrine of Ise|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine of Ise|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} is another name for either Ōmiyanome or [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]]. At shrines like [[:en:Yūtoku Inari Shrine|Yūtoku Inari Shrine]] and {{ill|Shiwa Inari Shrine|ja|志和稲荷神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiwa Inari Shrine|qq=}}, Ōmiyanome is identified with Ame-no-Uzume and revered as a deity of artistic skill.<ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=https://www.yutokusan.jp/about/|title=お祀りする神様|publisher=祐徳稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-15}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|language=ja|url=http://www.shiwa-oinarisan.jp/about/|title=志和稲荷神社について|publisher=志和稲荷神社|accessdate=2016-04-16}}</ref>
== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. Additionally, she is worshiped at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] and other [[:en:Inari shrine|Inari Shrines]] across Japan as a subordinate deity of Ukanomitama.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}
{{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 6]]
[[Category:Amatsukami]] }}
[[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E5%AE%AE%E8%B3%A3%E7%A5%9E]]

In the {{ill|Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja|大殿祭祝詞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōtono Hōgai Norito|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} found in the [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]], she is described as a deity who resides in the [[:en:Imperial Palace|Imperial Palace]] and ensures that the {{ill|Imperial princes|1=Shinnō|ja|親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial princes|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinnō|qq=}} and court ministers serve faithfully without error.

Her role is to mediate and harmonize the relationship between the emperor and his subjects.<ref name=tobe>{{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155|pages=271–272}}</ref>
As one of the {{ill|Three Deities of Inari|ja|稲荷三神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Three Deities of Inari|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135525231}} enshrined at [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]], she is worshiped as a subordinate deity to [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]], the main deity of the upper shrine.<ref name=inariookami/> Some interpretations suggest that she is a deified [[:en:Miko|miko]] who served the agricultural deity Ukanomitama.<ref name=tobe/> Because of her attributes, she was worshipped as the guardian deity of official markets in [[:en:Heian-kyō|Heian-kyō]], eventually becoming associated with prosperity in business.<ref name=inariookami/>

== Shrines Dedicated to Ōmiyanome == [[File:Omiya-inari-jinja (Kyoto, Kyoto).JPG|thumb|200px|right|Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine ([[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]])]] [[File:Omiyame-jinja, haiden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|{{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} (Kyōtango City)]] Ōmiya Hime Inari Shrine, located near the former site of the [[:en:Department of Divinities|Department of Divinities]] in [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], and {{ill|Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiya Shrine|lt=Ōmiyanome Shrine|ja|大宮売神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmiyanome Shrine|qq=}} in [[:en:Kyōtango|Kyōtango]], the only {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} bearing the name "Ōmiyanome," venerate her as the primary deity. == References == {{Reflist}}
== Further Reading == * {{Cite book|language=ja|editor=式内社研究会編|title=式内社調査報告 第1巻|publisher=[[:en:Kogakkan University|Kōgakkan University]]出版部|year=1979}} ''' {{Harvid|序説（式内社）}}, {{Harvid|御巫祭神八座（式内社）}}. * {{Cite book|author=岡田精司|title=新編 神社の古代史|publisher=学生社|year=2011|isbn=978-4311203022}} * {{Cite book|author=戸部民夫|title=「日本の神様」がよくわかる本|publisher=PHP研究所|year=2004|isbn=978-4569661155}} == See Also == * {{ill|Eight Kami of Jingikan|ja|八神殿|1=Hasshinden|1=Hasshinden|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Eight Kami of Jingikan|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshinden|qq=}} * [[:en:Ame-no-Uzume|Ame-no-Uzume]] * [[:en:Fushimi Inari-taisha|Fushimi Inari-taisha]] * [[:en:Inari Ōkami|Inari deity]] * [[:en:Ukanomitama|Ukanomitama]] * {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|サタヒコ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}
